subject: DevOps
title: Policy as Code
description: A fast breakdown of Policy as Code for builders.

hook.text: The policy said no public S3 buckets. It was a PDF nobody read. A dev made one public anyway.

buildup.text: Written policies get ignored. Policy as code enforces rules automatically. Violations are blocked.

discovery.text: Open Policy Agent evaluates Rego rules against K8s manifests, Terraform plans, and API requests.

twist.text: Policy as code is versioned and testable. Review, approve, and rollback policies like app code.

climax.text: Kyverno writes policies in YAML for Kubernetes. It validates and mutates resources to enforce rules.

punchline.text: Encode policies in code. Machines enforce rules better than docs.

quiz.question: What is the advantage of policy as code over written policy documents?
options: Policies are enforced automatically and violations are blocked, not just documented
Written documents are harder to create
Policy as code is only useful for small teams
